destinations.evidence: Canonical payload building opens parent state by the block hash being built on and passes the resulting EVM DB into heartbeat revival synthesis.
Heartbeat revival selection reads FAILED agents and per-agent revival data directly from the supplied parent-state DB, avoiding stale in-memory tracker state.

equivalence_argument: The old hydration-before-tracker-use invariant is preserved by a stronger local design: canonical building does not use the heartbeat tracker for revival selection at all; it reads the parent block state by hash and selects failed agents from contract storage.
notes: This is a structural mapping rather than a same-symbol mapping; tracker hydration is unnecessary because tracker state is bypassed in the canonical builder path.
rationale: The primary diff adds a tracker_needs_hydration check, obtains a state provider by block hash, calls sync_from_storage under a write lock if still unsynced, and only then reads failed agents from the tracker. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Before canonical payload building uses the heartbeat tracker to select failed or expired agents, it must hydrate the tracker from contract storage when tracker.is_synced(block_number) is false, using state_by_block_hash for the block being processed.
suggested_falsification_tests: Start with an unhydrated tracker and contract storage containing a failed agent, call canonical processing, and assert sync_from_storage is invoked before get_failed_agents and the failed agent is considered.
summary: Canonical heartbeat revival uses block-hash parent state instead of stale tracker state.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Populate parent-state heartbeat storage with a FAILED agent while the in-memory tracker is unsynced; canonical payload building should still synthesize the revival transaction from storage.
title: Canonical heartbeat revival hydrates before using tracker state
